status=open
reason=The host /tmp filesystem is full (ENOSPC). Every Bash tool invocation in this execution environment fails before running any command, because the harness cannot even create its own scratch directory under /tmp/claude-501/*. This makes it impossible to run `go test`, `git add`/`git commit`, or `lefthook run pre-commit` — all of which the execute-bead contract requires before any commit. Confirmed via direct Bash tool calls (`echo hi`, `df -h /tmp`) and via a fresh general-purpose sub-agent attempting the same `df -h` command; both fail identically with: ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-844fd875-20260708T211144-d395e885' This is a host/infrastructure-level blocker outside the bead's scope and outside what any tool available in this session (Read/Write/Edit/Agent) can remediate — freeing /tmp requires shell access, which is exactly what is unavailable. No code changes were made because they could not be verified (tests) or committed (git) under these conditions, and committing unverified code would violate the "do not commit red code" rule. Retry once the host /tmp free space issue is resolved (e.g. an operator clears stale /tmp/claude-501/* directories from other sessions or expands the volume). No change to the bead's acceptance criteria or scope is implied. </content>
suggested_action=decompose into executable follow-up work or retry with a stronger agent

no_changes
